steps.title: Place the interrupter
steps.content: The sentence says that relatively few particles reach Earth's surface, then uses however before a since-clause explaining why most particles burn up in the mesosphere. The since-clause depends on the earlier main clause, so it should remain in the same sentence. In this position, however is an interrupter inside one sentence, not the start of a new independent sentence. It therefore needs a comma before it and a comma after it.

steps.title: Avoid fragments and mispunctuation
steps.content: A uses a semicolon before however and then a period after it, leaving the since-clause as a fragment. C puts a semicolon after however, but what follows is a dependent since-clause, not an independent clause that can stand after a semicolon. D makes However start a new sentence, again stranding the since-clause without the main clause it explains. Commas on both sides of however keep the main clause and explanation connected while correctly marking the interrupter. The answer is B.
